babyLearning.en: growing rounder and ready, settling low for the journey ahead
babyLearning.hi: gol aur taiyaar hote hue, aage ke safar ke liye neeche bas rahe hain

title.hi: Aap Pehle Se Hi Kaafi Hain

insight.en: You do not need to become someone better before your baby arrives. The parent you already are is exactly enough.
insight.hi: Baby ke aane se pehle aapko koi behtar insaan banne ki zaroorat nahi. Jo parent aap pehle se hain, wahi bilkul kaafi hain.

expanded.en: As these last weeks draw close, it is natural to feel you should be more ready, more patient, more sure. But your baby is not waiting for a finished version of you. They are coming to the warm, willing, imperfect heart you carry right now. Enough does not mean perfect; it means present, loving and trying. Every quiet hope you hold, every gentle thought you have already given this little one — these are not the rehearsal for being a good parent. They are the proof you already are one.
expanded.hi: Jaise yeh aakhri hafte paas aate hain, yeh mehsoos karna sahaj hai ki aapko zyada taiyaar, zyada sabra wali, zyada sure hona chahiye. Par aapka baby aapke kisi poore ho chuke roop ka intezaar nahi kar raha. Woh us garm, taiyaar, adhoore dil ke paas aa raha hai jo aap abhi rakhti hain. Kaafi hone ka matlab perfect hona nahi; iska matlab hai maujood, pyaar bhara aur koshish karne wala. Har shaant ummeed jo aap rakhti hain, har naram vichaar jo aap is nanhe ko pehle hi de chuki hain — yeh achhe parent banne ki rehearsal nahi hain. Yeh saboot hain ki aap pehle se hi ek hain.

deepDive.en: Psychologist Donald Winnicott described the 'good enough' parent — one who responds with love most of the time, not perfectly. Children thrive on this realistic, warm care, not on flawlessness.
deepDive.hi: Psychologist Donald Winnicott ne 'good enough' parent ko bataaya — woh jo zyadatar samay pyaar se jawaab deta hai, perfect tareeke se nahi. Bachche isi vaastavik, garmjoshi bhari dekhbhaal par phalte-phulte hain, perfection par nahi.

remember.en: You are already enough for the baby who is choosing you.
remember.hi: Jo baby aapko chun raha hai, uske liye aap pehle se hi kaafi hain.

title.en: The Lark And Her Little Ones
title.hi: Chidiya Aur Uske Bachche

summary.en: A wise mother lark teaches her young that the help worth trusting is your own.
summary.hi: Ek samajhdaar chidiya apne bachchon ko sikhaati hai ki bharosa wahi madad layak hoti hai jo apni ho.

body.en:
A mother lark built her nest in a field of ripening wheat. Each day she flew off for food, telling her little ones to listen well and report whatever the farmer said. One evening the chicks were frightened: the farmer had told his son he would ask the neighbours to help cut the wheat tomorrow.

'Do not worry,' said the mother calmly. 'If he is waiting on neighbours, we have time.' Days passed and the neighbours did not come. Then the farmer said he would ask his relatives. Still the lark stayed calm. 'Relatives are slow too,' she smiled.

But one morning the chicks heard the farmer say, 'I shall cut the wheat myself, at dawn, with my own two hands.' That night the lark gathered her little ones. 'Now we must go,' she said, 'for when a person decides to do it themselves, the work truly begins.'
body.hi:
Ek maa chidiya ne pakte gehoon ke khet mein apna ghonsla banaaya. Har din woh khaane ke liye udti, apne bachchon se kehti ki dhyan se sunein aur jo bhi kisaan kahe woh bataayein. Ek shaam bachche dar gaye: kisaan ne apne bete se kaha tha ki woh kal gehoon kaatne ke liye padosiyon se madad maangega.

'Chinta mat karo,' maa ne shaanti se kaha. 'Agar woh padosiyon ka intezaar kar raha hai, to hamare paas samay hai.' Din beete aur padosi nahi aaye. Phir kisaan ne kaha ki woh apne rishtedaaron se maangega. Phir bhi chidiya shaant rahi. 'Rishtedaar bhi dheeme hote hain,' woh muskuraayi.

Par ek subah bachchon ne kisaan ko kehte suna, 'Main gehoon khud kaatunga, subah-subah, apne dono haathon se.' Us raat chidiya ne apne bachchon ko ikattha kiya. 'Ab humein jaana hoga,' usne kaha, 'kyunki jab koi khud karne ka faisla karta hai, tabhi kaam sach mein shuru hota hai.'

title.en: Tell your baby how they are already enough for you, just by being on their way.
title.hi: Apne baby ko bataayein ki woh sirf raaste mein hone se hi aapke liye pehle se kaafi hain.

motivation.en: Your baby learns their first sense of worth from how wholly they are wanted.
motivation.hi: Aapka baby apni keemat ka pehla ehsaas is se seekhta hai ki use kitni poori tarah chaaha gaya.

title.hi: Kaafi, Jaise Hum Hain

description.hi: Kaafi hone par ek chhota dhyan

affirmation.en: Right now, just as we are, you and I are already enough.
affirmation.hi: Abhi, jaise hum hain, tum aur main pehle se hi kaafi hain.

introduction.en: Settle into a comfortable seat and let your eyes close. Take three slow breaths and let your shoulders soften. Rest a hand on your belly and, for these few minutes, set down every thought of what you still need to do or become.
introduction.hi: Ek aaramdayak jagah par baithein aur aankhein band hone dein. Teen dheemi saansein lein aur apne kandhon ko naram hone dein. Ek haath pet par rakhein aur, in kuch minuton ke liye, woh har vichaar neeche rakh dein ki aapko abhi aur kya karna ya banna hai.

closingThought.en: Carry this with you: you did not need to fix a single thing to be enough for your baby today.
closingThought.hi: Ise apne saath le jaayein: aaj apne baby ke liye kaafi hone ke liye aapko ek bhi cheez theek karne ki zaroorat nahi thi.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar meditations are short, gentle practices to connect with your baby — not generic mindfulness, but a few minutes of sending warmth and love inward. Your calm becomes your baby's calm.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar meditations chhote, pyaare abhyaas hain apne baby se judne ke liye — koi aam mindfulness nahi, balki kuch minute garmjoshi aur pyaar andar bhejne ke. Aapki shaanti aapke baby ki shaanti ban jaati hai.

howToUse.en: Find a quiet spot, sit comfortably and close your eyes. Follow the introduction, breathe slowly, and rest your attention gently on your baby. Two to three minutes is plenty. Doing it daily, ideally at the same hour, deepens the bond.
howToUse.hi: Ek shaant jagah chunein, aaram se baithein aur aankhein band karein. Introduction follow karein, dheere se saans lein, aur apna dhyan pyaar se apne baby par tikaayein. Do se teen minute kaafi hai. Ise rozana, ho sake to ek hi samay karne se bond gehra hota hai.

title.hi: Saans Pet Tak Lein

content.en: Place both hands low on your belly. Breathe in slowly until you feel them rise, then let the breath sink all the way out. Five soft, low breaths settle the whole body.
content.hi: Dono haath pet par neeche rakhein. Dheere se saans lein jab tak aap unhe uthte mehsoos na karein, phir saans ko poori tarah neeche tak nikal jaane dein. Paanch naram, neeche tak ki saansein poore sharir ko sthir kar deti hain.

remember.en: A low, slow breath tells your body it is safe.
remember.hi: Ek neeche tak, dheemi saans aapke sharir ko batati hai ki woh surakshit hai.

babyLearning.en: practising tiny breaths, lungs growing strong and ready
babyLearning.hi: nanhi saansein abhyaas karte hue, phephde mazboot aur taiyaar hote hue

title.hi: Koi Perfect Parent Nahi Hota

insight.en: The perfect parent does not exist, and never has. Your baby does not need one — they need you.
insight.hi: Perfect parent ka koi astitva nahi hai, aur kabhi raha bhi nahi. Aapke baby ko koi perfect parent nahi chahiye — use aap chahiye.

expanded.en: Somewhere we picked up the idea that other parents have it figured out, and that we alone are fumbling. The truth is gentler: every parent, in every home, is improvising with love. The calm ones have hard days; the patient ones lose their patience. What looks like perfection from outside is simply people doing their honest best and beginning again. You can let go of the impossible standard now. It was never the measure. Showing up, soft and willing, over and over — that has always been the whole of it.
expanded.hi: Kahin se humne yeh soch utha li ki doosre parents ko sab samajh aa gaya hai, aur sirf hum hi ladkhada rahe hain. Sachai zyada naram hai: har parent, har ghar mein, pyaar ke saath improvise kar raha hai. Shaant logon ke bhi mushkil din hote hain; sabra wale apna sabra khote hain. Jo baahar se perfection lagta hai woh bas log apna imaandaar sabse achha karte aur phir se shuru karte hue hote hain. Aap ab us namumkin paimane ko chhod sakti hain. Woh kabhi maapne ki cheez tha hi nahi. Naram aur taiyaar, baar-baar maujood rehna — yahi hamesha se poori baat thi.

deepDive.en: Research on 'parental self-compassion' finds that parents who forgive their own mistakes respond to their children more warmly and consistently than those chasing perfection. Kindness to yourself becomes kindness to your child.
deepDive.hi: 'Parental self-compassion' par research paati hai ki jo parents apni galtiyon ko maaf karte hain woh apne bachchon ko perfection ka peecha karne waalon se zyada garmjoshi aur sthirta se jawaab dete hain. Khud par dayaalta aapke bachche par dayaalta ban jaati hai.

remember.en: Your baby needs a real parent, not a perfect one.
remember.hi: Aapke baby ko ek asli parent chahiye, perfect nahi.

title.en: The Mouse Who Became A Tiger
title.hi: Woh Chuhiya Jo Sherni Ban Gayi

summary.en: A wise hermit teaches that what we truly are matters more than the shape we are given.
summary.hi: Ek samajhdaar sant sikhaata hai ki hum sach mein jo hain woh us roop se zyada maayne rakhta hai jo humein diya jaata hai.

body.en:
A gentle hermit once found a tiny mouse fleeing a hungry cat. Out of kindness, he used his powers to turn the mouse into a strong cat. But soon the cat trembled before a dog, so the hermit made it a dog. Then the dog cowered from a tiger, and so, smiling, he made it a magnificent tiger.

The tiger grew proud and forgot all its old fears, until one day it heard the other animals whisper, 'Why should we bow? It is only a mouse the hermit turned into a tiger.' Furious and ashamed, the tiger decided to silence the hermit, who knew the truth of its small beginning.

But the wise hermit saw the tiger creeping near. With a quiet word, he gently returned it to its true mouse self. 'A grand shape,' he said softly, 'cannot hide a frightened heart. Peace comes not from seeming great, but from being at ease with who you are.'
body.hi:
Ek naram sant ne ek baar ek nanhi chuhiya ko ek bhookhi billi se bhaagte paaya. Dayaalta se, usne apni shaktiyon se chuhiya ko ek mazboot billi bana diya. Par jald hi billi ek kutte ke saamne kaanpne lagi, to sant ne use kutta bana diya. Phir kutta ek sher se darne laga, to muskuraate hue, usne use ek shaandaar sher bana diya.

Sher ghamandi ho gaya aur apne saare purane dar bhool gaya, jab tak ek din usne doosre jaanwaron ko fusphusaate suna, 'Hum kyun jhukein? Yeh to bas ek chuhiya hai jise sant ne sher bana diya.' Gusse aur sharm mein, sher ne us sant ko chup karaane ka faisla kiya jo uski chhoti shuruaat ka sach jaanta tha.

Par samajhdaar sant ne sher ko paas aate dekha. Ek shaant shabd se, usne use pyaar se uske asli chuhiya roop mein laut diya. 'Ek bada roop,' usne dheere se kaha, 'ek dare hue dil ko chhupa nahi sakta. Shaanti bada dikhne se nahi, balki jo aap hain usmein sehaj hone se aati hai.'

title.en: Tell your baby that they will never have to earn your love — it is already, fully theirs.
title.hi: Apne baby ko bataayein ki unhe kabhi aapka pyaar kamana nahi padega — yeh pehle se hi, poora unka hai.

motivation.en: A love that asks for nothing in return is the safest ground a child can stand on.
motivation.hi: Ek pyaar jo badle mein kuch nahi maangta, woh sabse surakshit zameen hai jis par ek bachcha khada ho sakta hai.

title.en: I Release The Need To Be Perfect
title.hi: Main Perfect Hone Ki Zaroorat Chhod Deti Hoon

affirmation.en: I lay down the burden of being perfect. My loving, willing heart is all you need from me.
affirmation.hi: Main perfect hone ka bojh neeche rakh deti hoon. Mera pyaar bhara, taiyaar dil hi sab kuch hai jo tumhe mujhse chahiye.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar affirmations are gentle, spiritually-rooted sentences a mother shares with her baby. The calm and love behind your words begin shaping a peaceful inner world for you both, long before your baby is born.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar affirmations woh pyaari, aadhyatmik vaakya hain jo ek maa apne baby se kehti hai. Aapke shabdon ke peeche ki shaanti aur pyaar, baby ke janm se bahut pehle hi, aap dono ke liye ek shaant andaruni duniya banane lagte hain.

howToUse.en: Sit quietly, rest a hand on your belly, and read the affirmation slowly — once silently, once aloud. Let yourself believe it for these few moments. A minute is enough, and repeating it at the same time each day makes it a calming ritual.
howToUse.hi: Shaanti se baithein, ek haath pet par rakhein, aur affirmation dheere se padhein — ek baar mann mein, ek baar bolkar. In kuch palon ke liye use sach maan lein. Ek minute kaafi hai, aur ise har din ek hi samay dohraana ise ek shaant ritual bana deta hai.

title.hi: Jab Seene Mein Jalan Ho

content.en: Late-pregnancy heartburn is common as your baby presses upward. Try smaller, more frequent meals, sip cool milk, and avoid lying down right after eating. Gentle, soothing food is kinder to a crowded belly now.
content.hi: Late-pregnancy mein seene ki jalan aam hai kyunki aapka baby upar ki taraf dabaata hai. Chhote, baar-baar wale meals lein, thanda doodh chuski se piyein, aur khaane ke turant baad letne se bachein. Naram, aaram dene wala khaana ab ek bhare pet ke liye zyada achha hai.

remember.en: Small meals are gentler on a body making room for two.
remember.hi: Chhote meals us sharir par zyada naram hote hain jo do ke liye jagah bana raha hai.

babyLearning.en: fingernails reaching the tips, soft and complete
babyLearning.hi: nakhun ungliyon ke siron tak pohanch rahe hain, naram aur poore

title.hi: Aapka Pyaar Hi Kaafi Yogyata Hai

insight.en: No one hands out a licence to be a parent. The love already in your heart is the only qualification that matters.
insight.hi: Parent banne ka koi license nahi bantata. Aapke dil mein pehle se mojood pyaar hi ekmaatra yogyata hai jo maayne rakhti hai.

expanded.en: You may worry you lack the right knowledge, the right calm, the right kind of patience. But parenting was never a test you must pass to qualify. The deepest qualification is the one you already hold: a heart that cares this much. Skills will come with time, and you will learn them in the doing, as every parent does. What cannot be taught — the willingness to love this child through everything — is the very thing you brought with you from the start. That is enough to begin, and more than enough to continue.
expanded.hi: Aap chinta kar sakti hain ki aapke paas sahi gyaan, sahi shaanti, sahi tarah ka sabra nahi hai. Par parenting kabhi koi test nahi thi jise paas karke aapko yogya banna ho. Sabse gehri yogyata woh hai jo aap pehle se rakhti hain: ek dil jo itna khayal karta hai. Kaushal samay ke saath aayenge, aur aap unhe karte-karte seekhengi, jaise har parent seekhta hai. Jo sikhaaya nahi ja sakta — is bachche ko har cheez ke saath pyaar karne ki taiyaari — wahi cheez aap shuru se apne saath laayi hain. Shuru karne ke liye yeh kaafi hai, aur jaari rakhne ke liye kaafi se zyada.

deepDive.en: Attachment researchers find that a caregiver's emotional availability — not their expertise — best predicts a securely bonded child. Love, reliably offered, is the foundation everything else rests on.
deepDive.hi: Attachment researchers paate hain ki ek caregiver ki bhaavnaatmak maujoodgi — uski expertise nahi — surakshit bonded bachche ko sabse achha bataati hai. Pyaar, bharose se diya gaya, woh neenv hai jis par baaki sab tika hota hai.

remember.en: Loving this much already qualifies you.
remember.hi: Itna pyaar karna hi aapko pehle se yogya bana deta hai.

title.hi: Sita Ki Andaruni Taakat

summary.en: Far from home, Sita shows that quiet inner steadiness is a strength no hardship can take.
summary.hi: Ghar se door, Sita dikhaati hain ki shaant andaruni sthirta ek aisi taakat hai jise koi mushkil chheen nahi sakti.

body.en:
When Sita was taken far from Rama and held in the gardens of Lanka, she was offered riches, comfort and a throne if only she would forget her own heart. All around her were threats and temptations, meant to break her spirit. Yet Sita sat quietly beneath a simple ashoka tree and would not bend.

She did not shout or despair. Her strength was a calm, unshakeable knowing of who she was and where her love belonged. When Hanuman came at last with word from Rama, he found her not crushed but steady, holding her dignity like a quiet flame the long months had never blown out.

Sita teaches a gentle truth: true strength is not loud or forceful. It is the deep, steady knowing of your own worth, which no hardship and no other voice can ever take away.
body.hi:
Jab Sita ko Ram se door le jaakar Lanka ke bagichon mein rakha gaya, to unhe daulat, aaram aur ek raj-singhasan ki peshkash ki gayi, bas agar woh apna dil bhool jaayein. Unke chaaron taraf dhamkiyaan aur lalach the, unka hausla todne ke liye. Phir bhi Sita ek saadi ashok ke ped ke neeche shaanti se baithi rahin aur nahi jhuki.

Unhone na chillaaya na nirash huin. Unki taakat ek shaant, na hilne wala gyaan tha ki woh kaun hain aur unka pyaar kahaan hai. Jab Hanuman aakhir Ram ka sandesh leke aaye, to unhone unhe toota hua nahi balki sthir paaya, apni garima ko ek shaant lau ki tarah thaame jise lambe maheenon ne kabhi nahi bujhaaya tha.

Sita ek naram sachai sikhaati hain: sachi taakat tez ya zorawar nahi hoti. Yeh apni keemat ka gehra, sthir gyaan hai, jise koi mushkil aur koi doosri aawaz kabhi chheen nahi sakti.

title.en: Tell your baby that your love for them comes with no conditions, today and always.
title.hi: Apne baby ko bataayein ki unke liye aapka pyaar bina kisi shart ke aata hai, aaj aur hamesha.

motivation.en: Love without conditions is the gift that lets a child grow up unafraid to be themselves.
motivation.hi: Bina shart ka pyaar woh tohfa hai jo ek bachche ko khud hone se dare bina bada hone deta hai.

title.hi: Raag Devgandhar

description.en: Morning raga for a gentle, devotional calm
description.hi: Subah ka raag, ek naram, bhakti bhari shaanti ke liye

affirmation.en: As this morning raga unfolds, a tender devotion rises in me, and reaches you.
affirmation.hi: Jaise yeh subah ka raag khulta hai, mujhmein ek komal bhakti uthti hai, aur tum tak pohanchti hai.

about.en: Ragas are classical Indian melodies, each tied to a mood and time of day. From about week 18 your baby can hear; even now, the calm a raga brings you surrounds your baby and gently shapes a peaceful environment around your pregnancy.
about.hi: Raag classical Bharatiya dhunein hain, har ek kisi mood aur din ke samay se judi. Lagbhag 18ve hafte se aapka baby sun sakta hai; abhi bhi, raag se aane wali shaanti aapke baby ko gher leti hai aur aapki pregnancy ke around ek shaant mahaul banati hai.

howToUse.en: Play the raga softly while you sit or rest with a hand on your belly. You needn't focus hard — let the sound fill the room as you breathe slowly. Two to five minutes, ideally at the raga's time of day, makes it a ritual your baby will one day recognise.
howToUse.hi: Raag dheere se chalaayein jab aap baithein ya pet par haath rakh kar aaram karein. Zyada dhyan lagane ki zaroorat nahi — aawaz ko kamre mein bhar jaane dein jab aap dheere saans lein. Do se paanch minute, ho sake to raag ke samay par, ise ek aisa ritual bana deta hai jise aapka baby ek din pehchaanega.

title.hi: Mera Pyaar Pehle Se Hi Kaafi Hai

content.en: I do not need to prove myself ready. The love I already carry is qualification enough for this child.
content.hi: Mujhe khud ko taiyaar saabit karne ki zaroorat nahi. Jo pyaar main pehle se rakhti hoon, woh is bachche ke liye kaafi yogyata hai.

remember.en: Your love was always the only qualification that counted.
remember.hi: Aapka pyaar hamesha se ekmaatra yogyata thi jo gini jaati thi.

babyLearning.en: turning towards your voice, learning the sound of home
babyLearning.hi: aapki aawaz ki taraf mudte hue, ghar ki aawaz seekhte hue

title.hi: Shaq Ka Matlab Naakaami Nahi

insight.en: Feeling unsure is not a sign you will be a bad parent. It is often a sign of how deeply you already care.
insight.hi: Anishchit mehsoos karna is baat ki nishaani nahi ki aap bure parent banengi. Yeh aksar is baat ki nishaani hai ki aap pehle se kitna gehraai se khayal karti hain.

expanded.en: As your due date nears, doubt may visit more often — am I ready, will I know what to do, what if I get it wrong. It is easy to read these questions as proof of unfitness. But the parents who never doubt are rarely the most attentive ones. Your doubt is your love asking to do well. It is the careful part of you, keeping watch. You can welcome the question without believing the worst answer. Caring enough to wonder if you are enough is, quietly, one more sign that you are.
expanded.hi: Jaise aapki due date paas aati hai, shaq zyada baar aa sakta hai — kya main taiyaar hoon, kya mujhe pata hoga kya karna hai, agar maine galti kar di to. In sawaalon ko apni naakaabiliyat ka saboot maanna aasaan hai. Par jo parents kabhi shaq nahi karte woh shaayad hi sabse dhyan dene wale hote hain. Aapka shaq aapka pyaar hai jo achha karna chahta hai. Yeh aapka savdhaan hissa hai, pehra dete hue. Aap sawaal ka swagat kar sakti hain bina sabse bure jawaab ko maane. Itna khayal karna ki aap sochein kya aap kaafi hain — yeh, chupchaap, ek aur nishaani hai ki aap hain.

deepDive.en: Studies on new parents show that mild self-doubt often pairs with greater sensitivity to a baby's needs. The reflective parent who asks 'am I doing right?' tends to attune more closely, not less.
deepDive.hi: Naye parents par studies dikhaati hain ki halka aatm-shaq aksar baby ki zarooraton ke prati zyada samvedansheelta ke saath jud-ta hai. Woh sochne wala parent jo poochta hai 'kya main sahi kar raha hoon?' zyada kareebi se attune karta hai, kam nahi.

remember.en: Your doubt is your care, wearing a worried face.
remember.hi: Aapka shaq aapka khayal hi hai, ek chintit chehra pehne.

title.hi: Nanhi Bater Ka Sach

summary.en: A newborn quail is saved not by strength, but by the quiet power of its own honest words.
summary.hi: Ek navjaat bater ko taakat se nahi, balki uske apne sachche shabdon ki shaant shakti se bachaaya jaata hai.

body.en:
In a forest nest lived a tiny quail chick, too young to fly or even walk. One day a great fire swept through the woods, and the flames roared closer and closer. The other birds flew up and away, but the little quail could only sit, helpless, in its nest.

The chick did not panic. Instead it spoke quietly and truthfully to the fire: 'I have wings, yet cannot fly. I have legs, yet cannot walk. My parents have flown to safety. By the truth of these honest words, I ask you to turn aside.'

And the great fire, it is said, stopped at the edge of the nest and rolled away, leaving the little quail unharmed. Sometimes our deepest safety lies not in being strong or able, but in the simple, fearless truth of who we are.
body.hi:
Ek jungle ke ghonsle mein ek nanha bater ka bachcha rehta tha, itna chhota ki na ud sakta tha na chal sakta tha. Ek din jungle mein ek badi aag faili, aur lapten paas aur paas garajne lagin. Doosre pakshi udd kar chale gaye, par nanhi bater bas apne ghonsle mein, bebas, baithi reh gayi.

Bachche ne ghabraaya nahi. Balki usne aag se shaanti aur sachai se kaha: 'Mere paas pankh hain, phir bhi ud nahi sakti. Mere paas pair hain, phir bhi chal nahi sakti. Mere maa-baap surakshit udd gaye hain. In sachche shabdon ke sach se, main tumse kehti hoon ki mud jao.'

Aur kehte hain, woh badi aag ghonsle ke kinare ruk gayi aur palat kar chali gayi, nanhi bater ko bina nuksaan ke chhod kar. Kabhi-kabhi hamari sabse gehri suraksha taakatwar ya saksham hone mein nahi, balki is saadi, nidar sachai mein hoti hai ki hum kaun hain.

title.en: Tell your baby how, again and again, you'll remind them that they matter.
title.hi: Apne baby ko bataayein ki kaise, baar-baar, aap unhe yaad dilaayengi ki woh maayne rakhte hain.

motivation.en: A child who is told they matter, often and warmly, carries that knowing for a lifetime.
motivation.hi: Jis bachche ko aksar aur garmjoshi se bataaya jaata hai ki woh maayne rakhta hai, woh us gyaan ko zindagi bhar le jaata hai.

title.hi: Shaq Ko Baithne Dena

description.en: A short meditation to soften worry
description.hi: Chinta ko naram karne ka ek chhota dhyan

affirmation.en: My worries can rise and pass, and underneath them, my love stays steady for you.
affirmation.hi: Meri chintaayein uth aur guzar sakti hain, aur unke neeche, mera pyaar tumhare liye sthir rehta hai.

introduction.en: Sit comfortably and close your eyes. Breathe slowly and picture your worries as leaves drifting down a quiet stream — you notice each one, then let it float on by. Beneath the moving water, feel a calm, steady love that does not move.
introduction.hi: Aaram se baithein aur aankhein band karein. Dheere saans lein aur apni chintaon ko ek shaant nadi mein behte patton ki tarah sochein — aap har ek ko note karti hain, phir use beh jaane deti hain. Behte paani ke neeche, ek shaant, sthir pyaar mehsoos karein jo hilta nahi.

closingThought.en: The doubts came and went, but your love did not waver once. That steadiness is yours to keep.
closingThought.hi: Shaq aaye aur gaye, par aapka pyaar ek baar bhi nahi dolaa. Woh sthirta aapki hai, sambhaal kar rakhein.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar meditations are short, gentle practices to connect with your baby — not generic mindfulness, but a few minutes of sending warmth and love inward. Your calm becomes your baby's calm.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar meditations chhote, pyaare abhyaas hain apne baby se judne ke liye — koi aam mindfulness nahi, balki kuch minute garmjoshi aur pyaar andar bhejne ke. Aapki shaanti aapke baby ki shaanti ban jaati hai.

howToUse.en: Find a quiet spot, sit comfortably and close your eyes. Follow the introduction, breathe slowly, and rest your attention gently on your baby. Two to three minutes is plenty. Doing it daily, ideally at the same hour, deepens the bond.
howToUse.hi: Ek shaant jagah chunein, aaram se baithein aur aankhein band karein. Introduction follow karein, dheere se saans lein, aur apna dhyan pyaar se apne baby par tikaayein. Do se teen minute kaafi hai. Ise rozana, ho sake to ek hi samay karne se bond gehra hota hai.

title.hi: Chinta Ko Saans Se Nikaalein

content.en: Breathe in for a count of four. As you breathe out for a count of six, silently let a single worry leave with the breath. Repeat for one quiet minute.
content.hi: Chaar tak ginte hue saans lein. Chhah tak ginte hue saans chhodte waqt, mann hi mann ek chinta ko saans ke saath jaane dein. Ek shaant minute tak dohraayein.

remember.en: A longer breath out gently tells the worry it can go.
remember.hi: Ek lambi saans baahar pyaar se chinta ko batati hai ki woh ja sakti hai.

babyLearning.en: soft and full-cheeked now, storing warmth for the world
babyLearning.hi: ab naram aur bhare gaalon wale, duniya ke liye garmjoshi sanjote hue

title.en: This Is What You're Here For
title.hi: Aap Isi Ke Liye Yahan Hain

insight.en: All the love you have been carrying was leading here. This — caring for one small person — is what you are here for.
insight.hi: Jo saara pyaar aap thaame chal rahi thi, woh yahin la raha tha. Yeh — ek chhote insaan ka khayal rakhna — isi ke liye aap yahan hain.

expanded.en: There are seasons in life when we wonder what it is all for. As your baby's arrival nears, a quiet answer may begin to settle in. The patience you built, the tenderness you saved, the love that had nowhere to fully go — it was all preparing for this. You will not be perfect at it, and you do not need to be. But you are made for this kind of love in a way no qualification could grant. When you hold your baby, something deep in you will recognise: this is the work my heart was shaped to do.
expanded.hi: Zindagi mein aise daur aate hain jab hum sochte hain ki yeh sab kis liye hai. Jaise aapke baby ka aana paas aata hai, ek shaant jawaab baithne lag sakta hai. Jo sabra aapne banaaya, jo komalta aapne sambhaal kar rakhi, jo pyaar jiske paas poori tarah jaane ki jagah nahi thi — woh sab isi ke liye taiyaari kar raha tha. Aap ismein perfect nahi hongi, aur aapko hone ki zaroorat nahi. Par aap is tarah ke pyaar ke liye bani hain, jis tarah koi yogyata nahi de sakti. Jab aap apne baby ko thaamengi, aapke andar kuch gehra pehchaan lega: yeh wahi kaam hai jiske liye mera dil gadha gaya tha.

deepDive.en: Researchers studying meaning in life find that caring for a child reliably ranks among the most profound sources of purpose people report. The sense that 'this is what I'm for' is real, and deeply human.
deepDive.hi: Zindagi ke arth par research karne wale paate hain ki ek bachche ka khayal rakhna bharose se un sabse gehre maksad ke srot mein aata hai jo log batate hain. Yeh ehsaas ki 'main isi ke liye hoon' asli hai, aur gehraai se insaani hai.

remember.en: Your heart was shaped for exactly this love.
remember.hi: Aapka dil theek isi pyaar ke liye gadha gaya tha.

title.hi: Birbal Aur Anmol Sevak

summary.en: Birbal shows that a person's true worth is not measured by their title or their pay.
summary.hi: Birbal dikhaata hai ki kisi vyakti ki sachi keemat uske ohde ya tankhwah se nahi naapi jaati.

body.en:
One day Emperor Akbar grew annoyed with an old palace servant who swept the floors and tended the lamps. 'He earns so little and does such humble work,' said the emperor. 'What true worth can such a man have?' Birbal said nothing, but asked for a day to answer.

The next morning Birbal quietly arranged for the old servant to take leave. By afternoon the lamps had gone unlit, the courtyards lay unswept, and a small fire from a fallen candle had nearly spread before others noticed. The smooth running of the palace had quietly come undone.

'Your Majesty,' said Birbal, 'his pay is small, but his worth is not the same thing. The value of a person is measured by what is missing when they are gone — and by that measure, he is priceless.' Akbar nodded, and never again confused a humble title with a small worth.
body.hi:
Ek din Badshah Akbar ek boodhe mehal ke sevak se chidh gaya jo farsh jhaadta aur deepak sambhaalta tha. 'Woh itna kam kamaata hai aur itna mamooli kaam karta hai,' badshah ne kaha. 'Aise aadmi ki bhala kya sachi keemat ho sakti hai?' Birbal kuch nahi bola, par jawaab dene ke liye ek din maanga.

Agli subah Birbal ne chupchaap us boodhe sevak ko chhutti dilwa di. Dopahar tak deepak bin jale rahe, aangan bin jhaade pade rahe, aur ek gire hue mombatti se chhoti aag doosron ke note karne se pehle lagbhag fail chuki thi. Mehal ka sahaj chalna chupchaap bigad gaya tha.

'Jahaanpanah,' Birbal ne kaha, 'uski tankhwah chhoti hai, par uski keemat woh cheez nahi hai. Ek vyakti ki keemat is se naapi jaati hai ki jab woh nahi hota to kya kami hoti hai — aur us paimane se, woh anmol hai.' Akbar ne sir hilaaya, aur phir kabhi ek mamooli ohde ko chhoti keemat se nahi mila.

title.en: Tell your baby that their worth has nothing to do with what they achieve.
title.hi: Apne baby ko bataayein ki unki keemat ka unki uplabdhiyon se koi lena-dena nahi.

motivation.en: A child whose worth feels safe and unearned grows free to try, to fail, and to be themselves.
motivation.hi: Jis bachche ki keemat surakshit aur bina kamaaye lagti hai, woh koshish karne, naakaam hone aur khud hone ke liye aazaad badhta hai.

title.en: I Was Made For This Love
title.hi: Main Is Pyaar Ke Liye Bani Hoon

affirmation.en: Everything in me has been preparing for you. I was made to love and hold a little one like you.
affirmation.hi: Mujhmein sab kuch tumhare liye taiyaari kar raha tha. Main tumhare jaise ek nanhe ko pyaar karne aur thaamne ke liye bani hoon.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar affirmations are gentle, spiritually-rooted sentences a mother shares with her baby. The calm and love behind your words begin shaping a peaceful inner world for you both, long before your baby is born.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar affirmations woh pyaari, aadhyatmik vaakya hain jo ek maa apne baby se kehti hai. Aapke shabdon ke peeche ki shaanti aur pyaar, baby ke janm se bahut pehle hi, aap dono ke liye ek shaant andaruni duniya banane lagte hain.

howToUse.en: Sit quietly, rest a hand on your belly, and read the affirmation slowly — once silently, once aloud. Let yourself believe it for these few moments. A minute is enough, and repeating it at the same time each day makes it a calming ritual.
howToUse.hi: Shaanti se baithein, ek haath pet par rakhein, aur affirmation dheere se padhein — ek baar mann mein, ek baar bolkar. In kuch palon ke liye use sach maan lein. Ek minute kaafi hai, aur ise har din ek hi samay dohraana ise ek shaant ritual bana deta hai.

title.hi: Jab Bhookh Kam Lage

content.en: As your baby fills more space, large meals can feel too heavy. Choose smaller, nourishing plates — a little dal, soft khichdi, fruit, a handful of nuts. Eating often and lightly keeps your energy steady without crowding a full belly.
content.hi: Jaise aapka baby zyada jagah bharta hai, bade meals zyada bhaari lag sakte hain. Chhoti, poshan bhari plates chunein — thodi dal, naram khichdi, phal, mutthi bhar nuts. Baar-baar aur halka khaana aapki energy sthir rakhta hai bina ek bhare pet ko aur bhare.

remember.en: Little and often is plenty when space is short.
remember.hi: Thoda aur baar-baar kaafi hai jab jagah kam ho.

babyLearning.en: resting deeply, growing patient for the day to come
babyLearning.hi: gehraai se aaram karte hue, aane wale din ke liye sabra seekhte hue

title.hi: Jo Aap Pehle Se Jaanti Hain Us Par Bharosa Karein

insight.en: Beneath all the books and advice, you already carry a quiet wisdom about your own baby. Learn to trust it.
insight.hi: Saari kitaabon aur salah ke neeche, aap pehle se hi apne baby ke baare mein ek shaant samajh rakhti hain. Us par bharosa karna seekhein.

expanded.en: So much advice will come your way, some helpful and some not, and it is easy to feel that everyone knows your baby better than you do. But no one has been this close to this child but you. The instinct that makes you pause, soothe, or simply hold — that is real knowledge, growing in you already. Information from outside has its place, but it works best resting on the quiet trust within. You are allowed to listen to your own knowing. More often than you expect, it will be exactly right.
expanded.hi: Bahut saari salah aapke paas aayegi, kuch madadgaar aur kuch nahi, aur yeh mehsoos karna aasaan hai ki har koi aapke baby ko aap se behtar jaanta hai. Par is bachche ke itne kareeb aap ke siva koi nahi raha. Woh sahaj-gyaan jo aapko rukne, dilaasa dene, ya bas thaamne par majboor karta hai — woh asli gyaan hai, jo aap mein pehle se badh raha hai. Baahar ki jaankaari ki apni jagah hai, par woh tab sabse achha kaam karti hai jab woh andar ke shaant bharose par tiki ho. Aapko apni khud ki samajh sunne ki ijaazat hai. Aapki ummeed se zyada baar, woh bilkul sahi hogi.

deepDive.en: Studies show mothers can recognise their own baby's cry within days of birth, and respond to subtle cues others miss. Your body and mind are already learning your child in ways no manual can teach.
deepDive.hi: Studies dikhaati hain ki maayein janm ke kuch hi din mein apne khud ke baby ki ro pehchaan leti hain, aur un baareek ishaaron par jawaab deti hain jo doosre chook jaate hain. Aapka sharir aur mann pehle se hi aapke bachche ko un tareekon se seekh rahe hain jo koi manual nahi sikha sakta.

remember.en: Your own quiet knowing is wiser than you think.
remember.hi: Aapki khud ki shaant samajh aapki soch se zyada samajhdaar hai.

title.hi: Woh Aurat Jiske Paas Kaafi Tha

summary.en: A poor woman discovers that contentment, not riches, was the treasure all along.
summary.hi: Ek gareeb aurat khojti hai ki santosh, daulat nahi, hamesha se khazaana tha.

body.en:
In a small village lived a woman with little money but a warm, full life. Her hut was modest, her meals were simple, yet she sang as she worked and slept soundly each night. A wealthy merchant nearby, who had everything yet rested poorly, could not understand her ease.

Thinking money was her secret want, he sent her a heavy bag of gold coins. At once her peace vanished. She lay awake guarding the gold, suspicious of every sound, anxious it might be stolen. Her songs stopped, and worry lined her face.

After three sleepless nights, the woman carried the gold back to the merchant. 'Take it,' she said gently. 'I had everything I needed before this came. I see now that having enough was never about having more — it was about knowing that what I had was already plenty.'
body.hi:
Ek chhote gaon mein ek aurat rehti thi jiske paas paisa kam tha par ek garm, bhari zindagi thi. Uski jhopdi mamooli thi, uska khaana saadaa tha, phir bhi woh kaam karte gungunaati aur har raat chain se soti. Paas hi ek ameer vyapaari, jiske paas sab kuch tha par neend nahi aati thi, uski sehajta samajh nahi paata tha.

Yeh sochkar ki paisa uski chhupi chaahat hai, usne use sone ke sikkon ki ek bhaari potli bhej di. Turant uski shaanti gaayab ho gayi. Woh raat bhar jaag kar sone ki rakhwaali karti, har aawaz se shankit, ghabraayi ki kahin chori na ho jaaye. Uske geet ruk gaye, aur uske chehre par chinta aa gayi.

Teen bin neend ki raaton ke baad, aurat sona wapas vyapaari ke paas le gayi. 'Ise le lo,' usne pyaar se kaha. 'Iske aane se pehle mere paas sab kuch tha jiski mujhe zaroorat thi. Ab main samajhti hoon ki kaafi hona kabhi zyada hone ke baare mein nahi tha — yeh jaanne ke baare mein tha ki jo mere paas tha woh pehle se hi bahut tha.'

title.en: Tell your baby that simply being themselves will always be plenty.
title.hi: Apne baby ko bataayein ki bas khud hona hamesha bahut hoga.

motivation.en: When a child knows that being themselves is enough, they never have to perform to feel loved.
motivation.hi: Jab ek bachcha jaanta hai ki khud hona hi kaafi hai, to use pyaar mehsoos karne ke liye kabhi dikhaava nahi karna padta.

title.hi: Raag Madhukauns

description.en: Night raga for a deep, restful calm
description.hi: Raat ka raag, ek gehri, aaram bhari shaanti ke liye

affirmation.en: As this night melody deepens, a restful trust settles over us both.
affirmation.hi: Jaise yeh raat ki dhun gehri hoti hai, ek aaram bhara bharosa hum dono par utar aata hai.

about.en: Ragas are classical Indian melodies, each tied to a mood and time of day. From about week 18 your baby can hear; even now, the calm a raga brings you surrounds your baby and gently shapes a peaceful environment around your pregnancy.
about.hi: Raag classical Bharatiya dhunein hain, har ek kisi mood aur din ke samay se judi. Lagbhag 18ve hafte se aapka baby sun sakta hai; abhi bhi, raag se aane wali shaanti aapke baby ko gher leti hai aur aapki pregnancy ke around ek shaant mahaul banati hai.

howToUse.en: Play the raga softly while you sit or rest with a hand on your belly. You needn't focus hard — let the sound fill the room as you breathe slowly. Two to five minutes, ideally at the raga's time of day, makes it a ritual your baby will one day recognise.
howToUse.hi: Raag dheere se chalaayein jab aap baithein ya pet par haath rakh kar aaram karein. Zyada dhyan lagane ki zaroorat nahi — aawaz ko kamre mein bhar jaane dein jab aap dheere saans lein. Do se paanch minute, ho sake to raag ke samay par, ise ek aisa ritual bana deta hai jise aapka baby ek din pehchaanega.

title.hi: Main Apni Khud Ki Samajh Par Bharosa Karti Hoon

content.en: I will listen to the quiet wisdom inside me. I know my baby in a way no one else can.
content.hi: Main apne andar ki shaant samajh ko sunungi. Main apne baby ko us tarah jaanti hoon jaise koi aur nahi jaan sakta.

remember.en: Your own knowing deserves a place at the table.
remember.hi: Aapki khud ki samajh ko bhi ek jagah milni chahiye.

babyLearning.en: fully formed and waiting, enough exactly as they are
babyLearning.hi: poori tarah bane aur intezaar karte hue, bilkul jaise hain waise kaafi

title.hi: Kaafi Ek Sundar Shabd Hai

insight.en: We are taught to chase 'more,' but 'enough' may be the kindest word of all — for your baby, and for you.
insight.hi: Humein 'zyada' ka peecha karna sikhaaya jaata hai, par 'kaafi' shaayad sabse dayaalu shabd hai — aapke baby ke liye, aur aapke liye.

expanded.en: The world keeps whispering that we should be more — do more, achieve more, become more. But somewhere in these final weeks, a softer truth waits to be heard: enough is not a settling, it is a homecoming. Your love is enough. Your effort is enough. The ordinary, imperfect care you will give, day after day, is enough. And one day you will pass this same gift to your child — letting them know that they, too, are enough exactly as they are. There is deep rest in that small, beautiful word.
expanded.hi: Duniya baar-baar fusphusaati hai ki humein zyada hona chahiye — zyada karna, zyada haasil karna, zyada banna. Par in aakhri hafton mein kahin, ek naram sachai sunne ka intezaar karti hai: kaafi koi samjhauta nahi hai, yeh ghar lautna hai. Aapka pyaar kaafi hai. Aapki mehnat kaafi hai. Woh aam, adhoori dekhbhaal jo aap denge, din-ba-din, kaafi hai. Aur ek din aap yahi tohfa apne bachche ko denge — unhe bataate hue ki woh bhi, bilkul jaise hain waise, kaafi hain. Us chhote, sundar shabd mein ek gehra aaram hai.

deepDive.en: Psychologists note that 'enough-ness' — the felt sense of being adequate without constant striving — is strongly linked to lower anxiety and warmer relationships. Naming it can quietly loosen the grip of perfectionism.
deepDive.hi: Psychologists note karte hain ki 'enough-ness' — bina lagaataar koshish ke paryaapt hone ka ehsaas — kam ghabraahat aur zyada garmjoshi bhare rishton se gehraai se juda hai. Ise naam dena chupchaap perfectionism ki pakad ko dheela kar sakta hai.

remember.en: Enough is not less — it is a quiet kind of fullness.
remember.hi: Kaafi hona kam nahi hai — yeh ek shaant tarah ka bharaapan hai.

title.en: The Little Stream That Reached The Sea
title.hi: Woh Nanhi Dhaara Jo Samundar Tak Pohanchi

summary.en: A small stream learns that it does not need to be a river to belong to the sea.
summary.hi: Ek chhoti dhaara seekhti hai ki use samundar ka hissa hone ke liye nadi banne ki zaroorat nahi.

body.en:
High in the hills, a little stream trickled down between the rocks. It often felt small and unimportant beside the great rushing rivers it heard about. 'I am so thin and slow,' it sighed. 'How could a tiny stream like me ever matter, or reach anywhere worth going?'

Still, it kept flowing the only way it knew — gently, steadily, around stones and through quiet valleys, never trying to be grander than it was. Other streams joined it here and there, and it welcomed them, but it never stopped being simply itself.

Then one bright morning, the little stream rounded a bend and the wide, shining sea opened before it. Without forcing or hurrying, it slipped softly into the vast water and became one with the whole ocean. It had not needed to be a mighty river after all. Flowing as itself, gently and without end, had always been enough to bring it home.
body.hi:
Pahaadon mein ooper, ek nanhi dhaara chattanon ke beech se behti thi. Woh aksar un badi tez nadiyon ke saamne chhoti aur mahatvaheen mehsoos karti jinke baare mein woh sunti. 'Main itni patli aur dheemi hoon,' woh aah bharti. 'Mujhe jaisi nanhi dhaara bhala kaise maayne rakh sakti, ya kahin pohanchne layak jagah jaa sakti?'

Phir bhi, woh us ek hi tareeke se behti rahi jo woh jaanti thi — narmi se, sthirta se, patthron ke around aur shaant ghaatiyon se hokar, kabhi apne se bada banne ki koshish kiye bina. Doosri dhaaraayein yahan-wahan us mein milin, aur usne unka swagat kiya, par woh kabhi bas khud hona band nahi karti.

Phir ek chamakti subah, nanhi dhaara ek mod ke around mudi aur chaudaa, chamakta samundar uske saamne khul gaya. Bina zor ya jaldi ke, woh narmi se us vishaal paani mein samaa gayi aur poore samundar ke saath ek ho gayi. Use aakhir mein ek vishaal nadi banne ki zaroorat nahi padi. Khud ki tarah behna, narmi se aur bina ant ke, hamesha se use ghar laane ke liye kaafi tha.

title.en: Tell your baby, softly, that they are enough — just exactly as they are.
title.hi: Apne baby ko, narmi se, bataayein ki woh kaafi hain — bilkul jaise hain waise hi.

motivation.en: These may be the truest words a child can carry: you are enough, just as you are.
motivation.hi: Yeh shaayad sabse sachche shabd hain jo ek bachcha sambhaal sakta hai: tum kaafi ho, bilkul jaise tum ho.

title.hi: Kaafi Mein Aaram

description.en: A closing meditation on fullness and peace
description.hi: Bharaapan aur shaanti par ek samaapan dhyan

affirmation.en: I have done enough, I am enough, and you are enough. Together, we can simply rest.
affirmation.hi: Maine kaafi kiya hai, main kaafi hoon, aur tum kaafi ho. Saath mein, hum bas aaram kar sakte hain.

introduction.en: Sit or lie back comfortably and let your eyes close. Take three slow, full breaths, letting your body grow heavy and at ease. For these few minutes, there is nothing to add and nothing to chase — only you, your baby, and the quiet sense that all of this is already enough.
introduction.hi: Aaram se baithein ya letein aur aankhein band hone dein. Teen dheemi, poori saansein lein, apne sharir ko bhaari aur sehaj hone dete hue. In kuch minuton ke liye, kuch jodne aur kuch peecha karne ko nahi hai — sirf aap, aapka baby, aur yeh shaant ehsaas ki yeh sab pehle se hi kaafi hai.

closingThought.en: You end this week held by a gentle truth: you and your baby are already, quietly, enough. Carry it into all the days to come.
closingThought.hi: Aap is hafte ko ek naram sachai mein thaame khatam karti hain: aap aur aapka baby pehle se hi, chupchaap, kaafi hain. Ise aane wale saare dinon mein le jaayein.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar meditations are short, gentle practices to connect with your baby — not generic mindfulness, but a few minutes of sending warmth and love inward. Your calm becomes your baby's calm.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar meditations chhote, pyaare abhyaas hain apne baby se judne ke liye — koi aam mindfulness nahi, balki kuch minute garmjoshi aur pyaar andar bhejne ke. Aapki shaanti aapke baby ki shaanti ban jaati hai.

howToUse.en: Find a quiet spot, sit comfortably and close your eyes. Follow the introduction, breathe slowly, and rest your attention gently on your baby. Two to three minutes is plenty. Doing it daily, ideally at the same hour, deepens the bond.
howToUse.hi: Ek shaant jagah chunein, aaram se baithein aur aankhein band karein. Introduction follow karein, dheere se saans lein, aur apna dhyan pyaar se apne baby par tikaayein. Do se teen minute kaafi hai. Ise rozana, ho sake to ek hi samay karne se bond gehra hota hai.

title.hi: Ek Poori, Sthir Karti Saans

content.en: Breathe in slowly and deeply until your lungs feel full, pause for a gentle moment, then release it all in one long, soft sigh. Just three of these can bring you home to now.
content.hi: Dheere aur gehri saans lein jab tak aapke phephde bhare na lagein, ek naram pal rukein, phir use ek lambi, naram aah mein poora chhod dein. Aise sirf teen aapko abhi mein wapas la sakti hain.

remember.en: One full breath is always enough to begin again.
remember.hi: Ek poori saans phir se shuru karne ke liye hamesha kaafi hai.
